# Black-box cross-validation fixtures

`blackbox_*.gsm` are reference encodes produced by an independent GSM
06.10 implementation invoked as an **opaque black box** (no source
consulted — binary in, bytes out), for the deterministic PCM programs
regenerated by `tests/blackbox_fixtures.rs` (`gen_amp` / `gen_hostile`
/ `gen_rand`, integer-only synthesis).

Generation commands (macOS/Homebrew binaries, 2026-07-11):

```sh
# <name>.raw = s16le mono 8 kHz PCM written by the in-test generators
sox -t raw -r 8000 -e signed -b 16 -c 1 <name>.raw -t gsm blackbox_<name>.gsm
```

| fixture | PCM program | frames |
|---|---|---|
| `blackbox_quiet.gsm` | `gen_amp(200, 1024, 512)` — §5.2.4 scaling never engages | 200 |
| `blackbox_loud.gsm` | `gen_amp(200, 8192, 2048)` — §5.2.4 scaling engages every frame | 200 |
| `blackbox_hostile.gsm` | `gen_hostile()` — full-scale squares, max-slew, `i16::MIN` plateau, impulses, chirp | 40 |
| `blackbox_rand.gsm` | `gen_rand()` — amplitude-swept LCG noise, loudest step clips at the rails | 300 |

The decoder-side digests pinned in `tests/blackbox_fixtures.rs` are
FNV-1a-64 over the little-endian PCM of the *reference* black-box
decode of the same fixtures:

```sh
sox -t gsm -r 8000 blackbox_<name>.gsm -t raw -e signed -b 16 <name>_dec.raw
```

Cross-checks performed when the fixtures were minted:

* Our §5.2 encoder reproduces every fixture **byte-for-byte** (and
  did so over 5 460 frames of additional material not checked in).
* Our §5.3 decoder matches the `sox` reference decode
  **sample-for-sample** on all four fixtures (480 000+ samples of
  additional material was also sample-exact).
* `ffmpeg -f gsm` (a second, unrelated black-box decoder) agrees with
  both on `quiet`, `loud`, and `hostile`; on `rand` (the clipped
  extreme stream) it diverges from the reference decode at
  saturation-heavy frames — two independent implementations against
  one, so the reference digest is the one pinned.

These files are validator *outputs* (transcodes of our own generated
test signals), not third-party media.
